name: elastic-notebook-slim
documentation:
# ElasticNotebook

Welcome to ElasticNotebook! ElasticNotebook is a checkpoint/restore tool for Jupyter Notebook sessions, which uses a 
novel live migration mechanism that is reliable, efficient, and platform-independent.

## Getting Started

### カレントディレクトリにダウンロードせずライブラリとして使う方法
```bash
pip install elastic-notebook-slim
```


### ソースコードをローカルのカレントディレクトリにダウンロードして使う方法
```bash
git clone https://github.com/MRyutaro/elastic_notebook_slim.git
pip install ./elastic_notebook_slim
```

## Project Structure

```
elastic-notebook
│   ElasticNotebook.py                     ## top-level cell magic
│───algorithm
│   │───baseline.py                        ##  Migrate/recompute all baselines
│   │───optimizer_exact.py                 ##  Replication plan generation via min-cut reduction (Section 5.3)
│   └───selector.py                        ##  Optimizer base class
└───core
    │───common
    │   │───checkpoint_file.py             ## Struct of ElasticNotebook's checkpoint file
    │   │───profile_graph_size.py          ## Size estimator for ElasticNotebook's Application History Graph
    │   │───profile_migration_speed.py     ## Profiler for network bandwidth
    │   └───profile_variable_size.py       ## Profiler for variable size
    │───graph
    │   │───cell_execution.py              ## Data structure for Cell Executions
    │   │───graph.py                       ## ElasticNotebook's Application History Graph (Section 4.1)
    │   └───variable_snapshot.py           ## Data strcutre for Variable Snapshots
    │───io
    │   │───adapter.py                     ## File writer base class
    │   │───filesystem_adapter.py          ## Writer for writing checkpoint file to NFS
    │   │───migrate.py                     ## Helper for creating checkpoint file
    │   │───pickle.py                      ## Helper for variable serializability detection
    │   └───recover.py                     ## Helper for unpacking checkpoint file
    │───mutation
    │   │───fingerprint.py                 ## Helper for detecting variable modifications via ID graph and object hash
    │   │───id_graph.py                    ## ID graph construction and comparison (Section 4.2)
    │   └───object_hash.py                 ## Object hash construction and comparison (Section 4.2)
    └───notebook
        │───checkpoint.py                  ## Line magic for checkpointing notebook session (Section 3.2)
        │───find_input_vars.py             ## AST analysis module for finding input variables
        │───find_output_vars.py            ## Helper for finding created/deleted variables via namespace difference
        │───restore_notebook.py            ## Line magic for restoring notebook session (Section 3.2)
        └───update_graph.py                ## Helper for updating Application History Graph
```

classes.name: elastic.algorithm.baseline.MigrateAllBaseline
documentation: Migrates all active VSs.

classes.name: elastic.algorithm.baseline.RecomputeAllBaseline
documentation: Recomputes all active VSs.

classes.name: elastic.algorithm.optimizer_exact.OptimizerExact

methods.name: elastic.algorithm.optimizer_exact.OptimizerExact.get_new_idx
documentation: Get a new index number to add to compute graph.

methods.name: elastic.algorithm.optimizer_exact.OptimizerExact.dfs
documentation:
Perform DFS on the Application History Graph for finding the CEs required to recompute a variable.
Args:
    current (str): Name of current nodeset.
    visited (set): Visited nodesets.
    recompute_ces (set): Set of CEs needing re-execution to recompute the current nodeset.

methods.name: elastic.algorithm.optimizer_exact.OptimizerExact.find_prerequisites
documentation: Find the necessary (prerequisite) cell executions to rerun a cell execution.

The exact optimizer constructs a flow graph and runs the min-cut algorithm to exactly find the best
checkpointing configuration.

classes.name: elastic.elastic_notebook.ElasticNotebook
documentation:
Magics class for Elastic Notebook. Enable this in the notebook by running '%load_ext ElasticNotebook'.
Enables efficient checkpointing of intermediate notebook state via balancing migration and recomputation
costs.

classes.name: elastic.algorithm.selector.OptimizerType
documentation: String representations of all implemented optimizers.

classes.name: elastic.algorithm.selector.Selector

methods.name: elastic.algorithm.selector.Selector.__init__
documentation:
Creates a Selector instance with a migration speed estimate. The dependency graph and active VS fields
must be populated prior to calling select_vss.

methods.name: elastic.algorithm.selector.Selector.select_vss
documentation:
Classes that inherit from the `Selector` class (such as `Optimizer` and various baselines)
    should override `select_vss`.

Returns:
    set(VariableSnapshot): a subset of active VSs selected to migrate based on various heuristics and
    algorithms.

The `Selector` class provides interfaces to pick a subset of active VSs to migrate based on
    various heuristics and algorithms.

functions.name: elastic.core.io.migrate.migrate
documentation:
Writes the graph representation of the notebook, migrated variables, and instructions for recomputation as the
specified file.

Args:
    graph (DependencyGraph): dependency graph representation of the notebook.
    shell (ZMQInteractiveShell): interactive Jupyter shell storing the state of the current session.
    vss_to_migrate (set): set of VSs to migrate.
    vss_to_recompute (set): set of VSs to recompute.
    ces_to_recompute (set): set of CEs to recompute post-migration.
    filename (str): the location to write the checkpoint to.
    udfs (set): set of user-declared functions.

modules.name: elastic.core.io.pickle
documentation: /usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Copyright 2021-2022 University of Illinois

functions.name: elastic.core.io.pickle.is_picklable
documentation: Checks whether an object is pickleable.

functions.name: elastic.core.io.pickle.is_exception
documentation: List of objects which _is_picklable_dill returns false (or crashes) but are picklable.

modules.name: elastic.core.io.recover
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Copyright 2021-2022 University of Illinois

functions.name: elastic.core.io.recover.resume
documentation:
Reads the file at `filename` and unpacks the graph representation of the notebook, migrated variables, and
instructions for recomputation.

Args:
    filename (str): Location of the checkpoint file.

    write_log_location (str): location to write file read runtime to. For experimentation only.
    notebook_name (str): notebook name. For experimentation only.
    optimizer_name (str): optimizer name. For experimentation only.

methods.name: elastic.core.io.filesystem_adapter.FilesystemAdapter.read_all
documentation: The following (read then decode) is faster vs. directly returning dill.load when network speed is low.

functions.name: elastic.core.common.profile_graph_size.profile_graph_size
documentation:
Profiles the in-memory size of a dependency graph of Elastic Notebook.
For experiments only.
Args:
    graph (DependencyGraph): dependency graph to profile.

classes.name: elastic.core.common.checkpoint_file.CheckpointFile
documentation: JSON representation of the notebook checkpoint.

functions.name: elastic.core.common.profile_migration_speed.profile_migration_speed
documentation:
The migration speed is the sum of read and write speed (since we are writing the state to disk, then
reading from disk to restore the notebook). The function should ideally be fast (<1 sec).
Args:
    dirname: Location to profile.

functions.name: elastic.core.common.profile_variable_size.get_total_size
documentation: Compute the estimated total size of a variable.

functions.name: elastic.core.common.profile_variable_size.profile_variable_size
documentation:
Profiles the size of variable x. Notably, this should recursively find the size of lists, sets and dictionaries.
Args:
    x: The variable to profile.

functions.name: elastic.core.notebook.find_output_vars.find_created_deleted_vars
documentation: Find created and deleted variables through computing a difference of the user namespace pre and post execution.

functions.name: elastic.core.notebook.checkpoint.checkpoint
documentation:
Checkpoints the notebook. The optimizer selects the VSs to migrate and recompute and the OEs to recompute, then
writes the checkpoint as the specified filename.
Args:
    graph (DependencyGraph): dependency graph representation of the notebook.
    shell (ZMQInteractiveShell): interactive Jupyter shell storing the state of the current session.
    selector (Selector): optimizer for computing the checkpointing configuration.
    udfs (set): set of user-declared functions.
    filename (str): location to write the file to.
    write_log_location (str): location to write component runtimes to. For experimentation only.
    notebook_name (str): notebook name. For experimentation only.
    optimizer_name (str): optimizer name. For experimentation only.

functions.name: elastic.core.notebook.update_graph.update_graph
documentation:
Updates the graph according to the newly executed cell and its input and output variables.
Args:
     cell (str): Raw cell cell.
     cell_runtime (float): Cell runtime.
     start_time (time): Time of start of cell execution. Note that this is different from when the cell was
         queued.
     input_variables (set): Set of input variables of the cell.
     created_and_modified_variables (set): set of created and modified variables.
     deleted_variables (set): set of deleted variables
     graph (DependencyGraph): Dependency graph representation of the notebook.

functions.name: elastic.core.notebook.restore_notebook.restore_notebook
documentation:
Restores the notebook. Declares variables back into the kernel and recomputes the CEs to restore non-migrated
variables.
Args:
    graph (DependencyGraph): dependency graph representation of the notebook.
    shell (ZMQInteractiveShell): interactive Jupyter shell storing the state of the current session.
    variables (Dict): Mapping from OEs to lists of variables defined in those OEs.
    oes_to_recompute (set): OEs to recompute to restore non-migrated variables.
    write_log_location (str): location to write component runtimes to. For experimentation only.
    notebook_name (str): notebook name. For experimentation only.
    optimizer_name (str): optimizer name. For experimentation only.

functions.name: elastic.core.notebook.find_input_vars.find_input_vars
documentation:
Capture the input variables of the cell via AST analysis.
Args:
    cell (str): Raw cell cell.
    existing_variables (set): Set of user-defined variables in the current session.
    shell (ZMQInteractiveShell): Shell of current session. For inferring variable types.
    shell_udfs (set): Set of user-declared functions in the shell.

classes.name: elastic.core.notebook.find_input_vars.Visitor
documentation: Node visitor for finding input variables.

functions.name: elastic.core.mutation.fingerprint.base_typed
documentation:
Recursive reflection method to convert any object property into a comparable form.
From: https://stackoverflow.com/questions/1227121/compare-object-instances-for-equality-by-their-attributes

functions.name: elastic.core.mutation.fingerprint.deep_equals
documentation:
Extended equality comparison which compares objects recursively by their attributes, i.e., it also works for
certain user-defined objects with no equality (__eq__) defined.

functions.name: elastic.core.mutation.fingerprint.construct_fingerprint
documentation: Construct a fingerprint of the object (ID graph + hash).

functions.name: elastic.core.mutation.fingerprint.compare_fingerprint
documentation: Check whether an object has been changed by comparing it to its previous fingerprint.

modules.name: elastic.core.graph.cell_execution
documentation: !/usr/bin/env python
-*- coding: utf-8 -*-
Copyright 2021-2022 University of Illinois

classes.name: elastic.core.graph.cell_execution.CellExecution

methods.name: elastic.core.graph.cell_execution.CellExecution.__init__
documentation:
Create an operation event from cell execution metrics.
Args:
    cell_num (int): The nth cell execution of the current session.
    cell (str): Raw cell cell.
    cell_runtime (float): Cell runtime.
    start_time (time): Time of start of cell execution. Note that this is different from when the cell was
        queued.
    src_vss (List[VariableSnapshot]): Nodeset containing input VSs of the cell execution.
    dst_vss (List[VariableSnapshot]): Nodeset containing output VSs of the cell execution.
A cell execution (object) corresponds to a cell execution (action, i.e. press play) in the notebook session.

functions.name: elastic.core.mutation.object_hash.construct_object_hash
documentation: Construct an object hash for the object. Uses deep-copy as a fallback.

classes.name: elastic.core.mutation.object_hash.NoneObj
documentation: Object representing none.

classes.name: elastic.core.mutation.object_hash.DataframeObj
documentation: Object representing a dataframe.

classes.name: elastic.core.mutation.object_hash.UnserializableObj
documentation: Object representing general unserializable class.

functions.name: elastic.core.mutation.id_graph.is_root_equals
documentation: Compare only the root notes of 2 ID graphs.

functions.name: elastic.core.mutation.id_graph.is_structure_equals_helper
documentation: BFS helper for recursively comparing ID graphs.

functions.name: elastic.core.mutation.id_graph.is_structure_equals
documentation: Compare 2 ID graphs for equality.

functions.name: elastic.core.mutation.id_graph.construct_id_graph_node
documentation:
Helper function for constructing an ID graph. Constructs an ID graph node and recurses into reachable objects
in BFS fashion.
Args:
    obj: object.
    visited (set): set of visited objects (for handling cyclic references).

functions.name: elastic.core.mutation.id_graph.construct_id_graph
documentation:
Construct the ID graph for an object.
Returns:
    graph_bytestring (bytestring): the serialized bytestring of the ID graph.
    visited: a set of all IDs of reachable objects in the graph. For checking object overlaps.

classes.name: elastic.core.mutation.id_graph.IdGraphNode

methods.name: elastic.core.mutation.id_graph.IdGraphNode.__init__
documentation:
Args:
    obj_id: id (memory address) of the object.
    obj_type: type of the object.
    child_nodes: other objects reachable from this object (i.e. class attributes, list/set members)
The IdGraph is used to model the reachable objects (their ids and types) from a given object.

classes.name: elastic.core.graph.graph.DependencyGraph

methods.name: elastic.core.graph.graph.DependencyGraph.__init__
documentation: Create a new dependency graph. Called when the magic extension of elastic notebook is loaded with %load_ext.

methods.name: elastic.core.graph.graph.DependencyGraph.create_variable_snapshot
documentation:
Creates a new variable snapshot for a given variable.
Args:
    variable_name (str): variable_name
    deleted (bool): Whether this VS is created for the deletion of a variable, i.e. 'del x'.

methods.name: elastic.core.graph.graph.DependencyGraph.add_cell_execution
documentation:
Create a cell execution from captured metrics.
Args:
    cell (str): Raw cell cell.
    cell_runtime (float): Cell runtime.
    start_time (time): Time of start of cell execution. Note that this is different from when the cell was
        queued.
    src_vss (List): List containing input VSs of the cell execution.
    dst_vss (List): List containing output VSs of the cell execution.

A dependency graph is a snapshot of the history of a notebook instance.
Nodesets and operation events are the nodes and edges of the dependency graph.

classes.name: elastic.core.graph.variable_snapshot.VariableSnapshot

methods.name: elastic.core.graph.variable_snapshot.VariableSnapshot.__init__
documentation:
Create a variable snapshot from variable properties.
Args:
    name (str): Variable name.
    version (int): The nth update to the corresponding variable.
    deleted (bool): Whether this VS is created for the deletion of a variable, i.e. 'del x'.

A variable snapshot in the dependency graph corresponds to a version of a variable.
I.e. if variable 'x' has been assigned 3 times (x = 1, x = 2, x = 3), then 'x' will have 3 corresponding
variable snapshots.
